generatedNote: Honest capability evidence registry. Statuses reflect VERIFIED reality, not aspiration. Under ADR 0056 the primary substrate is NATIVE WINDOWS (WSL2 reframed optional/future); the manual provider-login hard stop is already satisfied — Codex and Claude are installed AND authenticated natively on Windows (verified via official status commands; no tokens read; PII redacted). A10-W.6 (2026-06-30) upgraded Codex 0.101.0 → 0.142.4: 0.101.0 was a model/version dead-zone with the ChatGPT account (only gpt-5.5 offered, but 0.101.0 too old to run it; gpt-5/gpt-5-codex not offered on ChatGPT accounts). Claude stays 2.1.195. Mandatory windows_* capabilities must reach verified_real_environment (real-host OS behavior) or verified_real_provider (real CLI runs) before the final operational 1.0; a CI fixture is not sufficient. Source probe: `pnpm triforge:doctor` on Windows 11 (build 26200), NTFS, 2026-06-30. Updated as each A10-W PR lands.

evidence: pnpm triforge:doctor on Windows 11 (10.0.26200, x64): 18 checks, 0 blockers, 1 long-path warning
Node v24.12.0 / pnpm 11.5.0 / corepack 0.34.5 / Git 2.52.0.windows.1; PostgreSQL 18 service Running, localhost:5432 reachable; repo on C: NTFS
risks: LongPathsEnabled=0 (admin to flip); mitigate git ops with core.longpaths=true
capabilities.notes: A10-W.1: the native Windows toolchain (Node/pnpm/corepack/Git/PostgreSQL) is present and both providers are authenticated, verified by `pnpm triforge:doctor` on a real Windows 11 host. Scope: substrate/toolchain readiness only — the full product run is windows_integrated_product_e2e.

evidence: apps/api/src/execution/path/pathPolicy.ts (the WIRED write gate: Windows lexical pre-gate + case-insensitive containment)
apps/api/src/test/windowsPathPolicy.host.test.ts (standalone validateContainedPath on real NTFS: mklink /J junction escape, ADS, reserved names, traversal, .git) — green on Windows 11 26200

apps/api/src/test/pathPolicy.windows.host.test.ts (the WIRED PathPolicyEngine.checkWrite/checkRead on real NTFS: junction escape, ADS, reserved names, UNC, traversal, .git, case-insensitive containment) — green on Windows 11 26200
risks: post-validation tail-junction TOCTOU: a junction planted at a not-yet-existing tail segment AFTER the check is not caught (RR-2 residual; full TOCTOU hardening is future). Reparse points present AT check time are denied.

AppExecLink / cloud-placeholder reparse tags are not tag-inspected (symlink/junction/mount-point are covered via lstat); admin-only symbolic links are not exercised in the matrix (directory junctions are).
case folding is ASCII-correct (toLowerCase), an approximation of the NTFS $UpCase table — the containment guarantee assumes a system-controlled (ASCII) worktree root.
the forbiddenRoots layer is defense-in-depth only; under containment it is largely inert (a target outside the worktree is already denied).
capabilities.notes: A10-W.2: deny-by-default Windows containment — volume identity + canonical (realpath) resolution of the nearest existing ancestor + case-folded segment-boundary containment (never a raw startsWith). Blocks UNC/extended-length/device namespaces, ADS, reserved device names, trailing dot/space, and junction/reparse escapes present at check time (incl. a nonexistent child below a hostile junction), plus .git. An inaccessible existing segment fails safe (denied_reparse_point). Verified on a REAL Windows host (NTFS, real mklink /J junctions) for BOTH the standalone WindowsExecutionPlatform.validateContainedPath AND the WIRED write gate PathPolicyEngine (which actually authorizes provider writes via roleEnforcer/writableRun); POSIX behavior unchanged. CI (Linux) runs the cross-platform lexical matrix; the real-NTFS reparse behavior is the local Windows-host run. Scope: lexical + existing-chain canonical containment — not yet end-to-end through a real provider write (that is the A10-W.7 pilots).

capabilities.notes: A10-W.3: WorktreeManager on native Windows / NTFS — state root %LOCALAPPDATA%\TriForge, isolated git linked-worktrees OUTSIDE the working tree, one branch per run, NEVER main (protected-branch refusal), hardened git (hooks-off + isolated global config + no credential prompting), MAX_PATH mitigation (core.longpaths=true), junction/reparse-escape refusal (case-insensitive containment), collision prevention, disk-limit, stale detection + crash recovery, append-only audit. evidence: apps/api/src/platform/windowsJobObject.ts (PowerShell + in-box C# Add-Type P/Invoke: CreateJobObject + JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE + AssignProcessToJobObject + TerminateJobObject; no install, auditable source)

apps/api/src/test/windowsJobObject.host.test.ts — real-host chaos: child+grandchild reaped on cancel (kill-on-close), output capture + exit code, timeout reaping, idempotent double-cancel — green on Windows 11 26200
risks: the Job Object mechanism is delivered + verified via createManagedProcess; the real provider-adapter spawn path (NodeProcessRunner.killTree) still uses taskkill /T /F and is wired to the job in A10-W.6.

assignment race: a grandchild spawned in the brief window BEFORE the holder assigns the child is not in the job; mitigated by the layered taskkill /T /F fallback in reap() (the chaos test delays the grandchild past assignment to prove kill-on-close).

output-flood and explicit breakaway-attempt are not yet dedicated chaos cases (breakaway is denied by default — no JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_BREAKAWAY_OK set); CREATE_SUSPENDED race-free launch is a future hardening over the assign-after-spawn approach.
memory/CPU Job Object limits are not yet applied (structure supports them; not set).
capabilities.notes: A10-W.4: native Windows process-tree supervision via a Job Object with JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE — replacing the POSIX process-group dependency. A PowerShell job holder with embedded in-box C# (Add-Type P/Invoke; NO install, NO opaque binary) creates the job and assigns the spawned child; reaping the holder (or writing 'kill') closes the last job handle and the OS terminates the whole tree (children, grandchildren, detached). Empirically verified on a real Windows host: a sleeping grandchild is reaped on cancel — exactly the case taskkill /T can miss. taskkill /T /F is a documented FALLBACK only (layered). Single terminal event; separate stdout/stderr; output cap; timeout; idempotent cancel; PID-reuse-safe (reap via the job handle, not the PID).

evidence: apps/api/src/execution/command/commandPolicy.ts (Windows safe command policy: reg/sc/schtasks/netsh/icacls/Defender/... = privileged; del/format/rd = destructive; bitsadmin/certutil = network; cmd/powershell/pwsh/wscript/cscript/mshta + codex/claude = blocked shells/script-hosts)

Composed real-host layers (each verified_real_environment): windows_path_policy (W.2), windows_worktree_manager (W.3), windows_job_object_supervision (W.4); ADR 0055 in-process policy composition extended to Windows
risks: OS-level confinement (Windows ACL / restricted token / Defender Firewall rules) is NOT implemented in 1.0 — the boundary is the in-process policy composition, NOT an OS sandbox (RR-4, mirrors ADR 0055's 'WSL2 is not the sandbox'). ACL/restricted-token are not presented as perfect isolation.
no setrlimit on Windows; Job Object memory/CPU limits are available but not yet applied (RR).
network policy is deny-by-default at the command-policy layer (network category blocked), not yet an OS firewall rule.
capabilities.notes: A10-W.5: the Windows isolation boundary is the in-process LAYERED policy composition (mandate §9), verified on a real Windows host: isolated git worktree (W.3) + canonical path containment (W.2) + Job Object process-tree control (W.4) + env allowlist with credential stripping (createRestrictedEnvironment) + deny-by-default safe command policy (Windows shells/script-hosts/registry/service/scheduled-task/Defender/firewall/destructive denied) + audit. Per ADR 0055/0056 the OS itself is NOT trusted as a sandbox; ACL/restricted-token/firewall are honest residuals (RR-4), not claimed as perfect isolation. The 13-invariant matrix is realized across the per-layer real-host suites (path/worktree/job-object/command-policy).

Capacity never fabricated (unknown stays null); reserves protect critical phases; hard stop works without a balance signal; isBillingAuthoritative always false. OS-independent.

A10.9: every REQUIRES_VERIFICATION item is closed as verified / unknown-scheduled / unsupported. The cited REQUIRES_VERIFICATION_REGISTER.md is WSL2-framed and is SUPERSEDED by ADR 0056: the real-provider items are now tracked by the native-Windows windows_* real-provider capabilities in THIS registry (the authoritative live status). The WSL2 register is retained as the historical record.

A10.11 + A10-W.1: the release gate evaluates this machine-readable registry; final-operational readiness requires verified_real_provider for real-provider capabilities and verified_real_environment for real-host capabilities (requiresRealEnvironment). No false green.

evidence: governance gate; review findings; risk register
capabilities.notes: No open blocker/critical findings. Tracked in docs/context/TRIFORGE_RISK_REGISTER.md. The Windows adversarial review (34 vectors) lands in A10-W.5/A10-W.9.

risks: CI runs on Linux; a native-Windows CI lane is a follow-up
capabilities.notes: CI required check 'Validate' green on main; main clean.
